prompt.description: Text prompt. Capped at 1500 chars by the API; the /api/minimax-image route truncates anything longer.

aspect_ratio.description: Pre-set aspect ratio. Use this OR width+height, not both. The route picks one based on what arrived.

width.description: Image width in pixels. 512-2048, must be a multiple of 8. Route rounds DOWN to the nearest 8 to stay within range.

height.description: Image height in pixels. Same range as width.

response_format.description: url returns a signed Aliyun OSS URL valid for 24h; base64 returns the image bytes inline. The route always requests url and proxies through /api/proxy-image so the browser can load over https.

n.description: Number of images per request. Caps at 9 (more than every Leonardo model).

prompt_optimizer.description: When true, MiniMax rewrites the prompt server-side before generation. Best for short prompts (route enables it when prompt < 180 chars).

seed.description: Deterministic seed. Optional; route forwards when the caller supplies it.

rules: Auth: same MINIMAX_API_KEY as the vercel-ai chat provider.
No style_ids, no quality enum, no guidance — image-01 is a single-config endpoint with prompt_optimizer as its only quality lever.

Returned URLs are plain http:// (Aliyun OSS signature is scheme-locked); /api/minimax-image must wrap them through /api/proxy-image so the browser can load them on the https origin (mixed-content otherwise blocks silently — see MXIMG-001).
Returned URLs expire 24h after generation. /api/proxy-image's 1h-fresh / 7d-stale cache extends effective lifetime past expiry.

base_resp.status_code != 0 means the API rejected the request even though HTTP returned 200; map 1026/1027 to content-filter / policy-violation user errors, all other non-zero codes to a generic upstream-failure 502.

No moderation-retry path on the route — MiniMax surfaces filter rejections via status_code 1026/1027 and the client just shows the error (vs Leonardo's content-filter path which retries once with a rewritten prompt).
